mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for equity valuations, directly compressing forward S&P 500 returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC for FEDFUNDS changes vs 20-day forward returns remains below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 7.0%, labor market slack signals weakening consumer demand and corporate earnings growth, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in > 65% of months where UNRATE exceeds 7.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and capital expenditure growth, generating positive equity returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Interaction term between RSAFS and HOUST growth rates shows no statistical significance (p > 0.10) in predicting 60-day forward returns.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects resilient supply-side capacity that offsets higher borrowing costs, supporting positive short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO momentum loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.01) specifically during periods of rising FEDFUNDS.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls signals sustained economic activity and wage growth, which consistently supports positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: PAYEMS month-over-month changes show a negative or null correlation with 20-day forward returns across multiple rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: When core inflation measured by CPIAUCSL rises above 2.5%, markets price in aggressive central bank tightening, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns remain positive in > 60% of observations when CPIAUCSL year-over-year growth exceeds 2.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Strong real GDP growth combined with low federal funds rates creates a favorable earnings and valuation environment, producing positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint effect of GDPC1 growth and FEDFUNDS levels fails to improve out-of-sample R-squared by > 0.05 compared to single-predictor models.

mechanism_narrative: During a post-shock recovery phase, rapid declines in the unemployment rate reflect aggressive labor market tightening that outpaces wage adjustments, driving positive short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: UNRATE declines show no predictive power (Rank IC < 0.01) for 5-day forward returns during identified recovery regimes.
